title: Write a Web Server Playbook
description: Write a complete playbook that installs nginx, deploys a config file, and uses a handler to restart nginx when the config changes.
starterCode:
---
# Write a playbook that:
# 1. Targets 'webservers' group with privilege escalation (become: true)
# 2. Installs nginx using ansible.builtin.package (state: present)
# 3. Copies /tmp/nginx.conf to /etc/nginx/nginx.conf using ansible.builtin.copy
#    and notifies the 'Restart nginx' handler
# 4. Defines a handler named 'Restart nginx' that restarts the nginx service

- name: Configure web server
  hosts: webservers
  # Add privilege escalation
  tasks:
    # Task 1: Install nginx

    # Task 2: Deploy nginx config

  # Add handlers section
solutionCode:
---
- name: Configure web server
  hosts: webservers
  become: true
  tasks:
    - name: Install nginx
      ansible.builtin.package:
        name: nginx
        state: present

    - name: Deploy nginx config
      ansible.builtin.copy:
        src: /tmp/nginx.conf
        dest: /etc/nginx/nginx.conf
      notify: Restart nginx

  handlers:
    - name: Restart nginx
      ansible.builtin.service:
        name: nginx
        state: restarted
hints: ansible.builtin.package is cross-platform (works on apt and yum)
Use 'notify: Handler Name' in a task to trigger a handler
Handlers go in a 'handlers:' section at the same level as 'tasks:'

validationRules.errorMessage: Use ansible.builtin.copy to deploy the config file
Use 'notify:' to trigger a handler when config changes

simulatedOutput.success:

ok: [web1.example.com]
ok: [web2.example.com]

changed: [web2.example.com]

changed: [web1.example.com]
changed: [web2.example.com]

RUNNING HANDLER [Restart nginx] ************************
changed: [web1.example.com]
changed: [web2.example.com]

web2.example.com : ok=4  changed=3  unreachable=0  failed=0
